rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds are available
Write output dependency guarantees in component type annexes using only visible feature names
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the appropriate scope to maintain architectural integrity
Use component type annexes for type-level contracts and implementation annexes for implementation-specific bindings
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Preserve the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections from the input AADL model
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Write range comparisons using the pattern `eq range_value : bool = x >= min_value and x <= max_value;`
Avoid encoding AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
